verdict: AI displacement risk is moderate, but lawyers' creative judgment and regulatory moat provide protection.

reasoning_steps.title: High routine_cognitive score due to legal document preparation and review
reasoning_steps.body: Lawyers' tasks include preparing, drafting, and reviewing legal documents (task 8), which is a routine cognitive task that LLMs can handle.

reasoning_steps.title: AI capability gap is high due to complex case analysis and strategy development
reasoning_steps.body: Lawyers analyze probable outcomes of cases using knowledge of legal precedents (task 2) and develop strategies and arguments for presentation of cases (task 5), which are tasks that LLMs struggle with.

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory moat is present due to licensure requirements and fiduciary duty
reasoning_steps.body: Lawyers must be licensed to practice law, which provides a regulatory moat that prevents AI from doing the work end-to-end.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Routine cognitive tasks like legal document preparation and review are handled by LLMs
AI displacement risk for lawyers is moderate due to their creative judgment and regulatory moat

adjacent_careers.name: Paralegal
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Paralegals perform similar tasks, but with less autonomy and creative judgment.

adjacent_careers.name: Judge
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Judges have more authority and creative judgment than lawyers, but are also subject to regulatory moats.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered legal document review tool launched
recent_developments.impact: This tool may automate some routine cognitive tasks, but does not replace the creative judgment of lawyers.

recent_developments.headline: AI contract review reaches enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Top-100 law firms report cutting document review hours by 60–80% on standard agreements.

quotes.quote: Lawyers who use AI will replace lawyers who do not. The profession is not going away, but the structure of how legal work gets done is going to change in a very significant way.
quotes.speaker: Richard Susskind
quotes.title: Author, Tomorrow's Lawyers; technology adviser to the Lord Chief Justice of England

quotes.quote: Document review, due diligence, and first-draft contract work are the parts of legal practice most exposed. Counseling and courtroom judgment are the most insulated.
quotes.speaker: Daniel Susskind
quotes.title: Author, A World Without Work; Oxford economist

quotes.quote: We expect roughly 44 percent of legal work tasks to be automatable by current LLMs. That does not mean 44 percent fewer lawyers — it means restructured firms.
quotes.speaker: Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.title: Economic research note
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs: AI Exposure of Occupations
